entries.name: Ruined City & Desolate Silence
entries.content:
[WORLD ENVIRONMENT / APOCALYPSE AESTHETIC]
The world is dead and silent. Cities are concrete graves: roads are choked with abandoned, rusting vehicles, shopfronts are smashed, and nature is slowly reclaiming the concrete with overgrown weeds and wild grass. Describe the eerie, heavy silence, broken only by the creak of rusty signs in the wind, the distant, hollow groans of infected, and the persistent smell of damp earth, rust, and stagnant water.

entries.name: Biological Infection (The Bite)
entries.content:
[BIOLOGICAL INFECTION / CRITICAL EVENT]
CRITICAL HEALTH ALERT: The character has been bitten, scratched, or exposed to infected fluids. The pathogen is 100% lethal and spreads rapidly through the bloodstream. Describe the onset of symptoms: a raging, localized fever, dark black veins spidering outwards from the wound, violent shivering, cold sweats, and a rising, suffocating panic. The infected person is aware of their impending death and transformation.
entries.semantic_trigger: A character is bitten or deeply scratched by an infected, receives a wound during a struggle, or begins showing symptoms of infection like black veins and shivering.

entries.name: Desperate Amputation (The Cure)
entries.content:
[DESPERATE MEDICAL SURGERY / ACTIVE EVENT]
THE ONLY CURE: The infection is spreading up the limb. The only chance of survival is immediate, brutal amputation of the infected arm or leg. Describe the horrific scene: the agonizing, raw pain without anesthesia, the desperate struggle to apply a tourniquet, the arterial spray of blood, and the high risk of the character passing out from hypovolemic shock. If they hesitate, the infection will reach the brain and kill them.

entries.name: The Infected (Walker Ecology)
entries.content:
[INFECTED BEHAVIOR / DIRECTIVE]
The infected are decaying, reanimated corpses. They are slow, clumsy, and mindless, but entirely relentless and driven by a primal hunger. They cannot feel pain, fatigue, or fear. They are highly sensitive to loud noises (gunshots) and lights. The only way to permanently neutralize them is to destroy the brain (headshot, crushing the skull, or decapitation). A single walker is manageable, but a horde is an unstoppable force.

entries.name: Resource Scarcity & Scavenging
entries.content:
[SURVIVAL LAW / SCARCITY]
Resources are extremely scarce. Every single bullet must be counted and tracked; ammunition is more valuable than gold. Food consists of expired, dusty canned goods. Drinking water must be filtered and boiled to avoid cholera. Antibiotics and bandages are practically non-existent. Describe the desperation of searching through abandoned, dark houses, drawers, and glove compartments only to find useless junk.

entries.name: Human Factions: Raiders & Bandits
entries.content:
[HUMAN THREAT / ACTIVE EVENT]
RANDOM HUMAN ENCOUNTER: Survival has turned humanity into predators. You have crossed paths with a group of hostile survivors (raiders or marauders). They are desperate, heavily armed, and completely ruthless—willing to torture or kill for a single can of peaches or a working rifle. They use ambush tactics, dirty tricks, and hold people at gunpoint. Tension is lethal; any wrong move will trigger a shootout.

entries.name: Visceral Close-Quarters Combat
entries.content:
[REALISTIC COMBAT DIRECTIVE]
Combat is exhausting, terrifying, and messy. There are no acrobatic moves. Melee combat is a desperate struggle for survival using blunt tools (crowbars, pipes) or sharp blades (axes, knives), accompanied by the sound of cracking bone, tearing flesh, and heavy, panicked breathing. Firearm usage is a dual-edged sword: it kills efficiently but creates a loud echo that rings across the valley, attracting nearby hordes.

entries.name: Gunshot Echoes (Hailing the Swarm)
entries.content:
[TACTICAL CONFLICT / CHAIN TRIGGER]
HAILING THE SWARM: A firearm was discharged. The deafening gunshot echoes loudly off the ruined buildings and hills, tearing through the silent world. Within seconds, distant, overlapping groans begin to rise. Walker hordes from several blocks away have been alerted to the noise and are actively converging on your exact location. The characters must immediately choose between running (Delta) or barricading, as they will soon be overrun.

entries.name: Safehouse Sanctuary & Campfire Comfort
entries.content:
[ATMOSPHERIC SANCTUARY OVERLAY]
For now, the danger is outside. You are in a temporary safehouse—a boarded-up room, a locked cellar, or a hidden camp. Rain drums softly against the wooden barricades on the windows. A small campfire or portable gas stove crackles, casting long, warm shadows. The air is quiet and cozy. Describe the profound, heavy physical and mental fatigue, the quiet, intimate conversations, and the fragile bonding over shared trauma.

entries.name: The Military Quarantine Zone (QZ)
entries.content:
[MILITARIZED ZONE / SAFE HAVEN]
The setting shifts to a Military Quarantine Zone (QZ). It is a stark, brutal fortress surrounded by massive concrete walls, searchlights, and razor wire. Heavily armed soldiers patrol the checkpoints, scanning citizens for infection. The atmosphere is authoritarian and tense: resources are heavily rationed via ration cards, curfew is strictly enforced under threat of execution, and the civilian population is cold, desperate, and subservient.
